summary: Israel has conducted multiple airstrikes on Hezbollah command posts in south Lebanon, including large‑scale tunnel demolitions, while the Lebanese army prepares deployments under Israeli‑linked supervision. Iran’s foreign minister met Iraqi officials in Baghdad ahead of a mass funeral for Iran’s supreme leader, and Syria condemned recent Israeli incursions in Quneitra and Daraa. Drone activity continues over southern Lebanese villages.
key_points: Israel’s air force struck three Hezbollah command posts in the Nabatieh and Maydun areas of south Lebanon in response to attacks on its forces (Source: @New_security8200/202146).
A high‑intensity explosion is planned to demolish extensive Hezbollah tunnels in southern Lebanon, with authorities warning residents of strong blasts (Source: @pkpoi/143023).
IDF forces killed a Hezbollah militant after an encounter in the Dir Siran area of southern Lebanon, during which an Israeli officer was killed and another wounded (Source: @idf_telegram/24465).
Operation “Sof Pasuk” is destroying a 200‑meter long, 25‑meter deep underground Hezbollah infrastructure in the Majdal Zoun area of south Lebanon (Source: @inon_yttach/9202).
The Lebanese army will deploy to the villages of Zaatar al‑Gharb and Fron under direct supervision of a senior Israeli commander arriving from Israel (Source: @New_security8200/202144).
Iran’s foreign minister Abbas Araghchi met senior Iraqi officials in Baghdad to prepare a mass funeral for Ayatollah Khamenei (Source: @presstv/196065).
Syria’s Al‑Jolani regime condemned Israeli incursions and shelling in Quneitra and Daraa as violations of sovereignty (Source: @presstv/196073).

sections.executive_summary: Israel has intensified air operations against Hezbollah in south Lebanon, targeting command posts and extensive tunnel networks. The Lebanese army is set to move into western villages under direct Israeli oversight, raising the risk of broader confrontation. Regional diplomatic activity includes Iran‑Iraq meetings and Syrian condemnation of Israeli actions.

points: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi held meetings with senior Iraqi officials in Baghdad to coordinate preparations for a mass funeral for Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei, scheduled for the following month (Source: @presstv/196065).

The same minister’s visit follows a broader diplomatic push by Tehran to engage regional partners ahead of the funeral, though no specific security arrangements were disclosed (Source: @presstv/196065).

Iran’s internal expert council experienced a rare public dispute after over 60 members issued a statement on the Iran‑U.S. nuclear understanding, urging adherence to “red lines” and condemning alleged U.S. and Israeli involvement (Source: @asafroz/50822).
Iranian officials have not reported any direct military actions in the current conflict window, focusing instead on diplomatic engagements in Iraq (Source: @asafroz/50824).

The Israeli Air Force bombed three Hezbollah command posts in the Nabatieh and Maydun sectors of south Lebanon, citing retaliation for continued attacks on Israeli forces (Source: @New_security8200/202146).

IDF ground units from the Golani Brigade encountered a Hezbollah operative in the Dir Siran area; the clash resulted in the death of Captain David Hazut and a light injury to another soldier, followed by the elimination of the militant in a nearby building (Source: @idf_telegram/24465).

Operation “Sof Pasuk” is underway to destroy a 200‑meter long, over 25‑meter deep underground Hezbollah tunnel network in the Majdal Zoun region, described as a major infrastructure target (Source: @inon_yttach/9202).

A massive high‑intensity explosion is scheduled for the evening to demolish a large Hezbollah tunnel system in southern Lebanon, with local councils warning residents of possible seismic‑type alerts (Source: @pkpoi/143023).

Israeli officials announced that the recent breach of the security agreement and attacks on IDF forces prompted the latest airstrikes, emphasizing a continued commitment to neutralize all Hezbollah threats (Source: @New_security8200/202146).
The IDF reported that its multi‑dimensional unit previously targeted a Hezbollah launcher in south Lebanon, continuing a pattern of pre‑emptive strikes (Source: @New_security8200/202146).
No explicit operational updates regarding Gaza or the West Bank were present in the source material.

The Lebanese army is set to deploy in the western villages of Zaatar al‑Gharb and Fron under direct supervision of Israeli senior commander Admiral Brad Cooper, who is expected to travel from Israel (Source: @New_security8200/202144).
Drone activity has been reported over several villages in south Lebanon and the Bekaa Valley since early morning, indicating continued aerial surveillance (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373508).

Hezbollah tunnel demolition warnings have been issued to residents of the Upper Galilee, noting that large explosions could be heard and may trigger earthquake warning systems (Source: @manniefabian/58699).
Local Lebanese media noted that Israeli‑linked operations are focusing on dismantling underground Hezbollah infrastructure, raising the risk of civilian disruption (Source: @pkpoi/143023).
The Lebanese parliamentary speaker condemned a recent agreement signed in Washington between Lebanon and Israel, calling it an imposed set of dictates (Source: @presstv/196078).

Syria’s Al‑Jolani regime condemned Israeli incursions and shelling in the Quneitra and Daraa governorates, labeling them violations of Syrian sovereignty and international law (Source: @presstv/196073).

Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi met Iraqi officials in Baghdad to coordinate a mass funeral for Iran’s supreme leader, indicating heightened diplomatic activity between Tehran and Baghdad (Source: @presstv/196065).
Iraqi police released images of large sums of US dollars found in the home of a Shia‑aligned parliamentarian, suggesting internal anti‑corruption actions (Source: @kianmeli1/88227).
Iraqi security forces have conducted two consecutive days of operations in Kirkuk province targeting suspected corruption officials (Source: @naya_foriraq/80274).
No direct operational information concerning Gulf states was present in the source messages.

key_developments: Israel’s air force struck three Hezbollah command posts in Nabatieh and Maydun, marking a significant escalation in cross‑border strikes (Source: @New_security8200/202146).
A planned high‑intensity explosion will demolish a major Hezbollah tunnel network in southern Lebanon, with authorities warning of seismic‑type alerts (Source: @pkpoi/143023).
IDF forces killed a Hezbollah operative after an encounter in Dir Siran, during which an Israeli officer was killed and another wounded (Source: @idf_telegram/24465).
Operation “Sof Pasuk” is destroying a 200‑meter long, 25‑meter deep underground Hezbollah facility in Majdal Zoun (Source: @inon_yttach/9202).
Lebanese army units are set to deploy to Zaatar al‑Gharb and Fron under direct supervision of Israeli Admiral Brad Cooper (Source: @New_security8200/202144).
Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi met Iraqi officials in Baghdad to prepare a mass funeral for Ayatollah Khamenei (Source: @presstv/196065).
Syria condemned Israeli incursions in Quneitra and Daraa as violations of sovereignty (Source: @presstv/196073).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah’s underground infrastructure in south Lebanon remains a focal point for Israeli air and ground operations, with large‑scale demolitions increasing the risk of collateral damage and civilian displacement. The Lebanese army’s deployment under Israeli oversight could deepen internal Lebanese tensions and provoke broader militia responses. Syrian condemnation of Israeli actions signals potential for wider regional escalation, while Iran’s diplomatic outreach in Iraq underscores Tehran’s intent to maintain influence amid the conflict.
sections.regional_response: Iran is leveraging diplomatic channels in Iraq to consolidate support ahead of a high‑profile funeral for its supreme leader, while Syria publicly denounced Israeli incursions, hinting at possible retaliatory measures. Iraqi security actions against corruption indicate internal stabilization efforts, but no direct military response to the Israel‑Hezbollah clashes was reported. The Lebanese political establishment remains divided, with parliamentary leaders rejecting recent Israel‑Lebanon agreements.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of drone activity over southern Lebanese villages should be intensified, as sustained aerial surveillance may precede further strikes. The timing of large‑scale tunnel demolitions suggests coordinated planning between Israeli ground units and air assets. Lebanese army movements under foreign command warrant close observation for signs of command‑and‑control integration. Iranian diplomatic activity in Baghdad may foreshadow increased Iranian influence in post‑conflict negotiations.
